headline: PM's department employs 43,000 — more than 5 ministries combined
context: The Prime Minister's Department headcount reached 43,000 staff in 2025, exceeding the combined workforce of the Education, Health, Finance, Home Affairs, and Defence policy divisions.

cards.big: 43,000 people work in the PM's department — a shadow government
cards.sub: Headcount grew 28% since 2020 despite promises of civil service reform

cards.big: PMD's RM18.6 billion budget is Malaysia's largest single allocation
cards.sub: Exceeds entire budget of 8 individual ministries including Transport

cards.big: JAKIM, MAMPU, and 32 other agencies sit under PMD umbrella
cards.sub: Concentration of agencies reduces parliamentary scrutiny through single vote supply

cards.big: UK's Cabinet Office employs 8,200 staff for a larger population
cards.sub: Malaysia's PMD is five times larger relative to government size

cards.big: The PM's department has become a ministry of everything — accountable to no one specifically
Should PMD agencies be redistributed to line ministries for better oversight?
